title: The behaviour of --unsetlabel might be wrong
body:
Hi,

We are interested in using the **--unsetlabel** functionality to remove labels inherited from base image. But the way it currently behaves does not make it suitable for the purpose. It might be a bug, as it does not remove just the inherited labels from base image but all labels. This means the labels that are specified in the current Dockerfile/Containerfile and also those that are being set with --label parameter.

To provide an example. Let's say I have Dockerfile such as:
```
FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi:latest

LABEL name="my-image"
LABEL release="2025"

RUN dnf install -y git
```

The ubi8 base image also contains those labels. So when during build I do something like:

```
buildah build --unsetlabel name --unsetlabel release -t my-test-image .
```

The resulting image does not have the name and release labels, even though I feel like the labels specified in Dockerfile should be kept and not removed as the help suggests:

```
--unsetlabel strings    unset label when inheriting labels from base image
```

The --unsetlabel also takes precedence when doing:

```
buildah build --unsetlabel name --label name=my-label -t my-test-image .
```

Meaning the resulting image does not have the label name. All this means that there is no simple way to remove just the labels from the base image. If I wanted to do that, I have to parse all labels from base image, parse all labels in dockerfile, and then make sure to do --unsetlabel only on the difference of those two sets. This is not very practical for the purpose this functionality was meant to be used as originally requested in https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/5063

I tried this on the following versions:

- 1.37.1
- 1.39.2
- 1.40.0-dev

Could you please a have a look at it and fix it if possible? And/or perhaps could https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5061 be reopened?

comments.body:
This behavior looks like it was intended in the PR based on a comment: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5062#discussion_r1337560741.

With the current functionality, users (or build tools) will need to list all of the labels that are being added to the image so that they can be removed from the `--unsetlabel` argument. While this may be possible in some situations, it would be preferable if we could just define which labels we _don't_ want to inherit and then let the image build define any that it wants as normal.

I'd prefer an option to disinherit _all_ labels from the base image without having to list them and let the child set only the new labels it chooses through its containerfile (or explicit additions of --label).
@ralphbean, I think that should be considered a separate feature. That was the design of #5061. Even without that, I think that the behavior of `--unsetlabel` should be fixed.

`--unsetlabel=all` or `--unsetlabel=*`

All might be closer to what we use for --drop-cap=all and --add-cap=all.

Although the purest might complain about labels call `all`

> Even without that, I think that the behavior of --unsetlabel should be fixed.

@arewm  Could you elaborate on this , what do you think is wrong with `unsetlabel` :) . I think `unsetlabel` works how it is named, it just unsets label without making distinction about where it's coming from either from `Containerfile` or `Base Image`.

I think `unset` label should honor removing labels from both `Containerfile` and `BaseImage` because assume a case where a user copies a `Containerfile` from web or `Containerfile` is directly being used from `ReadOnly` URL, then they can remove certain labels using `Unsetlabel`.

So I think `unsetlabel` is working correctly, maybe what is needed here was implemented in this PR https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5061

My understanding of this problem is that `unsetlabel` effectively prevents that label from being set on the final image. I expected it to just unset the label from being inherited. 

In the PR, we discussed being able to unset _all_ labels from the image (i.e. https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5062#issuecomment-1739680799) which would also prevent any labels from being set. This extended behavior as discussed certainly doesn't seem to be an expected side effect.

@arewm  I am not sure if that can be done, the name `unsetlabel` clearly defines it's functionality via its name. If in future users come with a use-case like which is defined in this comment https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6098#issuecomment-2766411213 then it will become problematic to give them this specific feature. I think it's more beneficial to fix the docs of `unsetlabel` and have some like this available https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5061 for this specific use-case.

This comment is not about  the need for the new functionality. I have no clue about that.
I'm just commenting about the proposed user interface.

I think introducing a special value _all_ creates problems when writing documentation.
It's better to introduce a new option instead. I wrote a comment about this before
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/12100#issuecomment-952304555
@mkosiarc @arewm I have rebased my `--inherit-labels` branch, could you please try code from this branch https://github.com/flouthoc/buildah/tree/inherit-labels if it works I will open a PR.

@flouthoc it works great !

Tried
```
buildah build --inherit-labels=false -t my-test-image .
```
On the Dockerfile specified above, and the only labels are:

```
{
  "io.buildah.version": "1.40.0-dev",
  "name": "my-image",
  "release": "2025"
}
```
Thank you for confirmation, I will confirm with maintaince once and open a PR.
